interface.name: wl_display
interface.description: The connection's root object. Class documentation comes from the generator's wl_display template, not this text.

request.name: sync
request.description: Requests a sync callback: its {@code done} fires once all prior requests are processed.

event.name: error
event.description: Fatal server error; {@code object} may be null if the id is no longer live.

event.name: delete_id
event.description: The server confirmed an object id is dead and reusable.

interface.name: wl_registry
interface.description: The registry singleton: advertises and binds the compositor's globals.

request.name: bind

description.summary: bind an object to the display

request.description: Binds global {@code name} as {@code type} at {@code version}, returning the typed proxy.

event.name: global_remove
event.description: A global was removed; requests to objects bound from it will be ignored.

interface.name: wl_callback
interface.description: A one-shot completion callback. The server destroys the object after firing {@code done}; the connection retires its id via {@code wl_display.delete_id} — no client action needed.

interface.name: wl_compositor
interface.description: The compositor global: creates surfaces and regions.

interface.name: wl_shm
interface.description: The shared-memory global: creates pools from file descriptors.

request.name: create_pool
request.description: Creates a pool backed by {@code fd} (e.g. from {@link io.github.nhwalker.unixsocket.UnixSocketProvider#sharedMemory(long)}). The caller retains ownership of the handle; keep it open until {@link io.github.nhwalker.wayland.core.WaylandConnection#flush()} has returned.

event.name: format
event.description: A pixel format the compositor supports. Raw enum int for forward compatibility; decode via {@link WlShm.Format#lookup}.

enum.name: format
enum.description: Buffer pixel formats. {@code ARGB8888}/{@code XRGB8888} are 0/1; all others are DRM fourcc codes. A representative subset of wayland.xml's list — the generator emits the complete set from the XML.

interface.name: wl_shm_pool
interface.description: A shared-memory pool from which {@link WlBuffer}s are carved.

request.name: create_buffer

description.summary: create a buffer from the pool

request.description: Creates a buffer over {@code [offset, offset + height * stride)} of the pool.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the pool; buffers created from it keep their storage alive.

request.name: resize

description.summary: change the size of the pool mapping

request.description: Grows the pool (never shrinks) after the backing fd was ftruncated larger.

interface.name: wl_buffer
interface.description: Pixel content that can be attached to a {@link WlSurface}.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the buffer. The underlying storage (e.g. shm pool memory) is unaffected.

event.name: release
event.description: The compositor no longer reads the buffer; the client may reuse its storage.

interface.name: wl_surface
interface.description: An onscreen surface: content is supplied by attaching buffers and committing.

request.name: attach
request.description: Attaches {@code buffer} as the pending content; {@code null} removes the content.

request.name: frame
request.description: Requests a frame-done callback for the next commit.

request.name: set_opaque_region
request.description: {@code null} means the whole surface is non-opaque.

request.name: set_input_region
request.description: {@code null} means the whole surface accepts input.

event.name: enter
event.description: The surface now overlaps {@code output} (expected interface: wl_output).

event.name: leave
event.description: The surface no longer overlaps {@code output} (expected interface: wl_output).

interface.name: wl_region
interface.description: A rectangle set, used for surface input and opaque regions.

event.name: geometry
event.description: Raw enum ints for {@code subpixel}/{@code transform}; decode via the enum lookups.

event.name: mode

description.summary: advertise available modes for the output

event.description: {@code flags} is the bitfield decoded by {@link WlOutput.Mode#setOf(int)}.

enum.name: transform
enum.description: Entry names starting with a digit in the XML are prefixed with '_' (generator rule).

enum.name: mode
enum.description: Bitfield enum: decode with {@link #setOf}, encode with {@link WireEnums#mask}.
